audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Exodus / Shemot tabernacle construction text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for ark, cover, cherubim, table, lampstand, incense altar, anointing oil, incense.

verses.restored: Bezalel made ark of acacia wood: two and a half cubits its length, one and a half cubits its width, and one and a half cubits its height.
He overlaid it with pure gold inside and outside, and made for it gold molding all around.
He cast for it four gold rings upon its four feet, two rings on one side and two rings on other side.
He made poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with gold.
He brought poles into rings on sides of ark, to carry ark.
He made cover/atonement-cover of pure gold: two and a half cubits its length and one and a half cubits its width.
He made two cherubim of gold; of hammered work he made them from two ends of cover.
One cherub from this end and one cherub from that end; from cover he made cherubim from its two ends.
The cherubim spread wings upward, sheltering cover with their wings, and their faces each toward the other; toward the cover were faces of cherubim.
He made table of acacia wood: two cubits its length, one cubit its width, and one and a half cubits its height.
He overlaid it with pure gold and made for it gold molding all around.
He made for it rim of handbreadth all around and made gold molding for its rim all around.
He cast for it four gold rings and put rings on four corners that were on its four feet.
Close to rim were rings, housings for poles to carry table.
He made poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with gold, to carry table.
He made vessels on table: its dishes, pans, bowls, and jars to pour libations, of pure gold.
He made lampstand of pure gold; of hammered work he made lampstand, its base, stem, cups, bulbs, and blossoms from it.
Six branches went out from its sides, three branches of lampstand from one side and three branches from other side.
Three almond-blossom cups on one branch, bulb and blossom, and three almond-blossom cups on another branch, bulb and blossom; so for six branches going out from lampstand.
On lampstand itself four almond-blossom cups, its bulbs and blossoms.
A bulb under two branches from it, a bulb under two branches from it, and a bulb under two branches from it, for six branches going from it.
Their bulbs and branches were from it; all of it one hammered work of pure gold.
He made its seven lamps, its tongs, and firepans of pure gold.
From a talent of pure gold he made it and all its vessels.
He made incense altar of acacia wood: one cubit its length and one cubit its width, square, and two cubits its height; its horns from it.
He overlaid it with pure gold, its top, sides all around, and horns, and made gold molding all around.
He made two gold rings under molding on two sides, as housings for poles to carry it with them.
He made poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with gold.
He made holy anointing oil and pure fragrant incense, work of perfumer.

translation_notes: Construction fulfills the mountain pattern of Exodus 25 and 30.
Portable presence remains emphasized through poles for ark, table, and incense altar.
Lampstand retains almond/tree imagery and one hammered-work unity.
Bezalel is named as lead maker, preserving artisan agency.
safety_note: This chapter honors skilled craft as faithful execution of sacred pattern. Do not flatten technical repetition; the repetition shows command becoming embodied work.
